evals.prompt: I have this function that fetches users, validates them, sends emails, logs everything, and updates a cache all in one 200-line function. How should I improve it?
evals.expected_output: Suggests incremental refactoring following Kaizen principles: break into smaller functions one at a time, test after each change, avoid big-bang rewrite
expectations: Recommends incremental improvement rather than a complete rewrite
Suggests breaking the function into smaller, focused functions
Advises testing after each refactoring step
Follows the iterative refinement approach: make it work, make it clear, make it efficient
Does not try to solve everything at once
References or applies separation of concerns

evals.prompt: I'm building a new API endpoint for user registration. I want to handle every possible edge case upfront: rate limiting, email verification, phone verification, social login, 2FA setup, and account linking. What's the right approach?
evals.expected_output: Advises against over-engineering, recommends starting with the simplest version that works and adding features incrementally based on real needs (JIT/YAGNI)
expectations: Warns against building everything at once (JIT/YAGNI principle)
Recommends starting with basic registration that works
Suggests adding features incrementally based on actual requirements
Applies the 'simplest thing that works' principle
Does not encourage premature optimization or premature abstraction

evals.prompt: Users keep entering invalid dates in our form and it crashes the backend. How should I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Applies Poka-Yoke (error proofing) principles: validate at the boundary, use types to make invalid states impossible, fail fast with clear messages
expectations: Suggests input validation at the system boundary
Recommends using types or validated types to prevent invalid data from propagating
Advises failing fast with clear, helpful error messages
Suggests making invalid states unrepresentable where possible
